scope: Compact agent-usability gap report for current data files after API examples, LibMoonCat surfaces, task recipes, routing updates, IPFS/CID verification, identifier-boundary cleanup, color-source inventory, color-model review, derived human-facing color classification, identifier conversion verification, materialization parity fixtures, coding-agent query benchmark/context packs, MoonCatColors internals review, original rescue-mining reference work, MoonCatSVGs internals review, MoonCatAccessoryImages internals review, the exact MoonCatsWrapped identifier review, the complete pinned dev-environment ADR directory index, and a compact pinned ChainStation implementation-surface audit.

method.summary: Reviewed requested files, status fields, TODO arrays, limitations, direct data/agent-index.json routing coverage, and current data/*.json inventory.
limitations: This file does not add MoonCat facts or verify upstream freshness.
This file does not modify sourceRefs, import datasets, or resolve listed gaps.
Routing coverage is checked against direct data/agent-index.json references in primaryFiles and optionalFiles.

notes: data/task-recipes.json is directly routed by data/agent-index.json.
data/kb-gap-index.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json through the review-kb-gaps task.
data/rescue-mining.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json through the understand-rescue-mining task.
data/mooncat-svg-internals.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json through render-images and look-up-contracts.
data/mooncat-accessory-images-internals.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json through render-images and look-up-contracts.
data/older-wrapper-internals.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json through identify-mooncat-ids and look-up-contracts.
data/chainstation-surfaces.json is directly routed by data/agent-index.json through trace-chainstation-implementation.
Current route candidates are directly discoverable from data/agent-index.json.

resolvedOrPartiallyResolvedGaps.notes: All 18 decision files in the pinned MoonCatRescue dev-environment ADR directory are indexed with byte-checked source paths, status, source update date, and intent-versus-implementation limits. The snapshot does not claim current-master freshness, decision dates, authors, unspoken supersession, implementation completion, deployment, or live state.
Agents now have compact checked and manifest-derived API examples; examples still do not replace endpoint manifests or OpenAPI schemas.
Observed helper names and conservative input/output surfaces are indexed without importing source code or full mappings.

Previous empty hue/special/pale structures are replaced by ADR-aligned hue buckets, aggregate observed snapshot data, color source inventory, MoonCatColors internals summaries, and guardrails; per-cat mappings, exact RGB/hex palettes, full renderer behavior, BasePalette values, and mapColors override values remain out of scope.

A deterministic, source-backed policy now maps normal integer hues to ADR-shifted human-facing labels and handles Genesis black/white sentinels before circular hue handling. Raw hue fields remain untouched, pale remains a separate modifier, and palette/rendering claims remain out of scope.

Representative rescueOrder/catIdBytes5 round trips now cross-check LibMoonCat and mooncat_traits.json, Acclimated tokenId equivalence is verified only for the exact contract, and WMCR/accessory/palette/owned-record/batch/marketplace identifier conflations have explicit negative or unsupported fixtures.

Eight fixed fixtures now verify explicit rescueOrder/catIdBytes5 identity, source-trait parity, and deterministic parser structure while retaining five documented trait differences. Per-cat MoonCatColors palette output, exact SVG serialization, and accessory composition remain explicitly unavailable, documented-only, or deferred rather than inferred.

Thirteen realistic coding-agent cases now generate minimal route-backed packs with explicit warnings, forbidden claims, stop conditions, commands, and size metrics. The suite validates routing/context contracts and dangerous-conflation coverage without network or subjective model scoring.
Common agent workflows now have load order, outputs, guardrails, and stop conditions, and the recipe file is routed from data/agent-index.json.
Rescue bucket status now records method/timestamp verification; identifier conversion and downstream-use boundaries still need caution.

data/ipfs-cids.json now records eight official MoonCatRescue source-observed CIDs/artifact folders and a supporting sourceRef; payload availability, file counts, schemas, and hash validation remain out of scope.
data/kb-gap-index.json is now directly discoverable from data/agent-index.json through the review-kb-gaps route.

The exact MoonCatsWrapped / Wrapped MoonCatsRescue contract is now verified at 0x7C40c393DC0f283F318791d746d894DdD3693572. Its WMCR token IDs are counter-allocated and mapping-backed to bytes5 cat IDs; the source contains no rescueOrder conversion. Other historical or unofficial wrappers remain outside this exact-contract review and must not inherit its behavior.

Color sources and claims are now inventoried separately from canonical color data, including ADR 0017, parser/source references, MoonCatColors surfaces, official guide context, MidnightLightning Reddit/Discord explanations, and observed trait snapshot context.

MoonCatColors internals now record compact verified semantics for colorsOf ordering, deriveColors ordering summary, accessoryColorsOf composition, paletteOf composition, mapColors purpose/application, hue helpers, and colorAlpha bands. Exact BasePalette values, mapColors keys/values, generated palettes, and per-cat outputs remain intentionally unimported.

The curated model decision is implemented as a 64-row generated visual-trait prototype keyed by bytes5 catId with rescueOrder as a source-backed secondary lookup field. The full trait table, frequencies, names, and accessory details remain unimported.

A compact 28-file ChainStation source snapshot now pins master commit 5df03ab465d46f59f3e4d4a05e4e85fc90abdc0a and indexes ten application/data surfaces with exact evidence paths, source/configuration/runtime classifications, ADR-intent separation, benchmark coverage, and offline hash/shape validation. It intentionally excludes full-repository coverage, external responses, secrets, deployment verification, and live state.

Important active reference inputs now have local SHA-256/size checks, dependency mappings, copy/adaptation status, revision/date/license evidence, and explicit freshness limitations. The upstream commit/tag and retrieval date remain unresolved for core local reference files where checked-in evidence does not support a stronger claim.

The exact MoonCatAccessories contract now has a compact source-backed review of definitions, management, rescue-order assignment, OwnedAccessory storage, eligibility, metadata verification, Acclimated approvals, alteration/discontinuation, administration, identifier boundaries, and MoonCatAccessoryImages consumption. Full accessory data and current chain state remain unimported.

A deterministic 64-row representative sample now provides source-backed per-cat visual traits, catId-primary and rescueOrder-secondary boundaries, per-field provenance categories, LibMoonCat comparisons, explicit mismatches, parser renderability checks, and dedicated validation. Full-population coverage and live freshness remain open.
A zero-dependency read-only Python validator now checks data/*.json parsing, required routed file references, kb-gap sourceFiles, sourceRef consistency, and relatedFiles warnings.

Original browser rescue/mining behavior is now documented from the reviewed local mooncatrescue-web mirror files public/scan.html, public/js/mine.js, and public/js/mine-worker.js, cross-checked against data/protocol-constants.json. Wallet-free snippet and embeddable widget examples were added without importing frontend bundles, original image assets, Web3, workers, or transaction submission behavior; the widget now renders found catIds through the registered mooncatparser.js reference.

MoonCatSVGs internals now record compact verified semantics for source identity, dependency interfaces, imageOf overload behavior, rescue-order conversion, default glow selection, SVG string assembly, output wrapper properties, and conceptual geometry/pattern surfaces. Full Solidity source, ABI JSON, coordinate constants, pattern arrays, generated SVG outputs, per-cat outputs, and accessory image composition remain intentionally unimported.

MoonCatAccessoryImages internals now record compact verified semantics for source identity, rescue-order entrypoints, explicit versus lookup accessory lists, ownership preparation, foreground/background handling, glow branches, PNG data-URI assembly, and dependency boundaries. Full Solidity source, ABI JSON, accessory taxonomy, placement tables, palettes, IDAT data, PNG bytes, generated SVG or PNG outputs, per-cat outputs, and current accessory state remain intentionally unimported.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for locating selected MoonCat detail, filtering, API, wallet, rendering, Firebase, and build/deployment evidence without loading the full app; it must not be used as a live system report.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review an additional source area only through a separately scoped pinned snapshot, or run an explicitly authorized live verification for deployment/API/chain/gateway questions.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as a compact endpoint manifest, but agents should not treat it as full OpenAPI schema or verified runtime behavior for every response edge case.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Re-sync from registered OpenAPI sources or live-check selected endpoints only in a focused API pass.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for constructing common requests, but some examples are placeholders and examples do not replace the endpoint manifest or OpenAPI specs.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add more checked examples only when a concrete workflow needs them, without storing response bodies.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for exact community-curated membership arrays, but agents need the index convention before downstream UI/tool use.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add or adjust memberships only from sourced community data and preserve the rescue-order-index boundary.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for category definitions and notes, but incomplete for exact memberships and premium counts.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Keep definitions separate from membership arrays and add missing counts only from sourced data.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for ADR-aligned hue buckets, aggregate observed hue/color context, compact MoonCatColors internals, parser/on-chain surface guidance, and guardrails. Still not a per-cat color table, exact RGB/hex palette source, full renderer spec, or generated image source.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Only add exact palette values, mapColors overrides, or per-cat mappings through a dedicated generated-data or storage-review pass with repeatable validation.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for choosing contract/source surfaces and now includes a compact MoonCatColors reviewed-internals pointer, but it is still not a full call-signature, ABI, source, bytecode, or storage-layout index.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: If exact call signatures or artifact details are needed, create a separate ABI/artifact policy instead of expanding this routing file.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for verified address/source identity and compact MoonCatSVGs/MoonCatAccessoryImages internals, but not for imported artifact blobs, unreviewed materialization dependencies, or current chain state.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review additional materialization dependencies only in focused source passes before importing derived behavior or artifact references.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for current identifier warnings, verified lookup-backed bytes5/rescue-order boundaries, current Acclimated token ID boundaries, and the exact MoonCatsWrapped mapping-backed wrapper rule. Additional historical wrapper contracts remain unresolved.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Identify additional historical or unofficial wrapper addresses only through exact high-confidence sources, then review each contract separately before adding mappings.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for source-observed MoonCatRescue CID/artifact references, but not for payload availability, file counts, metadata schemas, pinning status, or hash validation.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Optionally verify selected CID payload availability with HEAD/range-safe checks in a focused pass without importing payload bodies.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as an audit report and now directly discoverable from data/agent-index.json, but it can become stale after source or data passes.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Refresh this gap index after future substantial data/source imports.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for helper discovery and conservative input/output routing, but not full source-code documentation or canonical behavior proof.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review specific helper behavior only when a task needs implementation details beyond the surface index.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as preserved navigation/research links, but not a canonical fact source by itself.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Promote any link to sources.json only in a source-focused pass with trust and limitation notes.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Detects local snapshot drift and distinguishes pinned archive review evidence from unresolved upstream lineage, but does not prove the origin of every local copy.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Only improve individual entries when direct upstream revision, retrieval-date, or license evidence is available; do not invent missing provenance.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Provides 64 validated per-cat visual-trait rows with explicit provenance and mismatch reporting, but must not be used as complete population coverage.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Decide whether scale needs justify a full generated artifact, sharding, build-only output, and a snapshot update policy.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Provides exact source-level accessory lifecycle, role, verification, integration, and identifier semantics without current definition or ownership data.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Only inspect current state, accessory definitions, or event history for a concrete consumer and through a separately scoped provenance/update policy.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for top-level type scaffolding, but agents must keep community-curated categories separate from official protocol traits.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Clarify derivation methods and identifier conversions before using bucket/type values outside their source context.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for distinguishing official, historical, community, third-party, and ownership-unverified project/navigation links without promoting them into canonical sources.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Recheck only high-value ambiguous links in a dedicated verification pass; preserve unknown ownership and do not infer status from branding or URL shape.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for source-derived constants, but not enough to infer rescue ranges, category membership, or parser behavior beyond recorded constants.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Verify parser source and MD5 workflow before deriving image-generation or trait data.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for existing bucket arrays with improved method context, but values still need identifier-boundary checks before API, token, or bytes5 use.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Use data/identifier-conventions.json and task recipes before applying bucket values outside rescue-order-index contexts.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for original browser searchSeed, random seed candidate generation, keccak valid-hash check, normal catId derivation, rescueCat boundary, a wallet-free snippet, and an embeddable wallet-free widget that renders found catIds with mooncatparser.js; not useful for submitting rescues, current ownership, current availability, trait mapping, or broad parser-behavior claims.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Only expand this area with focused source review or current-chain checks; do not import the original frontend, rescued-cat dataset, Web3, workers, unrelated parser behavior, or transaction code to the examples.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as range and method scaffolding, but early-rescue meanings and some operational boundaries remain underspecified.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Define early-rescue ranges by rescue order, block, timestamp, or community convention before using them operationally.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Core source index is usable, but source/method entries for derived data and future exact values remain incomplete.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add derived data entries only after documenting the generating source or method.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as special-category scaffold, but exact memberships and protocol/community boundaries are not fully materialized.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Separate canonical protocol categories from community-curated categories and add exact membership only after source verification.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for sequencing common tasks, but recipes can drift if underlying data files change without a workflow refresh.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Refresh recipes after future curated trait, image, color, API, or source imports change accepted workflows.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for trait field schema, validation metadata, and the implemented bounded per-cat prototype model, but not for full-population lookup, frequencies, names, or accessory details.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Evaluate full-population need and update cadence using the prototype's documented scaling and mismatch constraints.

notableNonGaps.note: Routes all current data/*.json files except itself; data/kb-gap-index.json is routed through review-kb-gaps.
Fills the previous agent need for small API construction examples while preserving example limitations.
Fills the previous helper-surface discoverability gap without importing source code.
Provides workflow sequencing and guardrails for common agent tasks.

recommendedNextPasses.reason: The ADR index is complete only for pinned dev-environment commit d6a604e1a37fb5646d7e5059c0858bd15f9b7b83. Refresh only through a deliberate source/revision/license review; do not infer missing numbers, current-master content, or implementation completion.

The exact MoonCatsWrapped contract is reviewed, but the KB does not claim a complete inventory of historical or unofficial MoonCat wrappers. Any additional wrapper requires its own exact address and source review before adding mappings.
data/sources.json remains incomplete for some future exact values and derived-data methods.
data/ipfs-cids.json has source-observed CIDs, but payload availability, file counts, schemas, pinning status, and hash validation remain intentionally unchecked.
